methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $500M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Distalmotion
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series G)

data.narrative:
Wandelbots, a Series G robotics and manufacturing software company founded by Christian Piechnick, operates within a competitive landscape of 9 private peers (including Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics) and 1 public comparable. The company has raised $126M to date across its funding rounds.

The valuation analysis applied one applicable method: stage-progression analysis. This method uses the company's $500M total-raised proxy as a base and applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $625M. The peer-median method was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none yielded 3+ datapoints when filtered by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier, making sector-wide medians unreliably broad for this frontier-stage asset.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $531M (low) to $719M (high), with a median of $625M. This single-method range reflects meaningful uncertainty: the absence of tightly-matched peer multiples leaves stage-progression as the primary anchor, and the validity of the $500M base proxy itself remains a key assumption. Investors should weight this analysis alongside qualitative assessment of Wandelbots' market position relative to the identified peer set and underlying assumptions about Series G expansion multiples in robotics software.
